data.narrative:
Vannevar Labs, a Series B defense and security technology company founded by Brett Granberg and Nini Moorhead, is currently valued at $1.5B post-money on $90M of total capital raised. The valuation analysis draws from a comparable universe of 10 companies (2 public, 8 private) operating in the same sector and development stage, including Dream Security, Harmattan AI, Code Metal, Castelion, and Defense Unicorns.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis applies a 1.9x multiplier typical of Series B advancement to the $1.5B last post-money valuation, yielding $2.9B. Peer-median valuation across seven same-sector, same-stage comparables yields $1.0B, with the peer set ranging from $300M to $1.5B. These peers include Dream Security and Harmattan AI valued at $1.5B and $1.4B respectively, down to lower-quartile entrants at $300M.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $850M to $3.3B, with a median of $1.9B. The current $1.5B valuation sits slightly below the median implied value, positioning the company at the lower end of stage-appropriate pricing relative to near-term progression expectations but above the peer median. Key uncertainties include the relative maturity and revenue profile of peer companies versus Vannevar Labs, which the available data does not specify.
